title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: May 4, 2026 - Market Trends and Key Movers

items.headline: Tech Sector Surges as Q1 Earnings Impress; Broader Market Mixed Amid Geopolitical Concerns
items.summary: U.S. equities saw a mixed start to the week, building on Friday's record closes for the S&P 500 and Nasdaq, largely driven by robust corporate earnings, particularly from the technology sector. Gains were concentrated in mega-cap AI names, narrowing market breadth. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, coupled with high oil prices, continue to be a significant wildcard, though oil prices saw some fluctuation today following news of a U.S. initiative to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Investors are closely watching upcoming economic data and a busy week of earnings reports.

items.statContext: Last close (May 1, up 0.29%). Futures were nearly unchanged on May 4.
Last close (May 1, up 0.89%), a record high. Futures slipped 0.05% on May 4.
Last close (May 1, down 0.31%). Futures shed 0.3% on May 4.

items.headline: Apple Leads Gains on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Apple's stock surged after the company delivered better-than-expected fiscal Q2 earnings and reported 17% revenue growth, leading the S&P 500 higher. Improved revenue guidance was a key catalyst.

items.headline: Alphabet Soars on AI-Driven Cloud Revenue
items.summary: Alphabet experienced a significant jump, driven by robust enterprise AI demand which propelled a massive 63% year-over-year increase in its cloud revenue.

items.headline: Shuttle Pharmaceuticals Holdings Plummets
items.summary: Shuttle Pharmaceuticals Holdings Inc. was among the day's biggest losers, experiencing a substantial decline in its stock price.

items.headline: Meta Platforms Declines Despite Earnings Beat
items.summary: Meta Platforms' shares plunged despite beating earnings estimates, as investors scrutinized the company's lack of significant AI-related growth to justify massive capital expenditures and increased debt for buildout.

items.headline: Q1 2026 Earnings Season Exceeds Expectations
items.summary: The first-quarter 2026 earnings season is past the halfway mark, with approximately 80% of S&P 500 companies reporting profits up roughly 31% year-on-year, significantly surprising analysts to the upside. This week is particularly busy with over 100 S&P 500 companies, including Advanced Micro Devices ($AMD) and Palantir ($PLTR), scheduled to release their reports. Palantir ($PLTR) is reporting after market close today, with high expectations surrounding its commercial Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) contract growth.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict and Oil Prices Remain Key Market Drivers
items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly concerning the Strait of Hormuz, remains a significant factor influencing global markets and oil prices. Oil prices saw a rebound today, with Brent crude climbing over $2 a barrel to $110.40, following reports of a U.S. initiative, dubbed 'Project Freedom,' to guide ships through the Strait. However, Iran has reportedly rejected the plan. High oil prices continue to weigh on consumer confidence.

items.headline: Fed Maintains Rates Amid Divisions, Non-Farm Payrolls Awaited
items.summary: The Federal Reserve concluded its FOMC meeting on April 28-29, opting to keep interest rates steady at 3.5-3.75%. Notably, four members dissented, marking the highest level of internal disagreement in over three decades, indicating differing views within the Fed regarding future policy. Markets are currently pricing in the Fed holding steady through mid-year. Looking ahead, investors are keenly awaiting the April Non-Farm Payrolls report on Friday, with consensus expecting 49,000 jobs added and unemployment at 4.3%.

items.headline: Tech and Cyclicals Lead Amid AI Spending Scrutiny
items.summary: The technology, communication services, and consumer discretionary sectors are currently leading market performance, driven by strong earnings and investor rotation into economically sensitive names. This shift suggests a potential 'soft-ish landing' scenario for the economy. However, concerns persist regarding the substantial scale of AI capital expenditure, projected at $751 billion for 2026, and whether it will deliver the expected return on investment. Microsoft ($MSFT) and Meta ($META) experienced declines due to concerns over their AI spending versus revenue guidance.

items.headline: Not Financial Advice
items.summary: This market digest is for informational purposes only and does not constitute specific investment advice. Please consult with a qualified financial professional before making any investment decisions. The information provided is based on publicly available data and market observations as of May 4, 2026.
